Package neqsim.pvtsimulation.flowassurance

Class DeWaardMilliamsCorrosion

java.lang.Object
neqsim.pvtsimulation.flowassurance.DeWaardMilliamsCorrosion
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class DeWaardMilliamsCorrosion extends Object implements Serializable
CO2 corrosion rate prediction using the de Waard-Milliams (1975/1991) model.

The de Waard-Milliams model is the industry-standard empirical correlation for estimating internal CO2 corrosion rates in carbon steel pipelines carrying wet gas or multiphase fluids. It is referenced in NORSOK M-506 and widely used in subsea pipeline flow assurance studies.

The 1991 revised model gives corrosion rate as: 

 
 log10(Vcor) = 5.8 - 1710 / (T + 273.15) + 0.67 * log10(pCO2)

where Vcor is in mm/yr, T in degrees Celsius, pCO2 in bar.

Correction factors can be applied for:

  • Protective FeCO3 scale (at high temperature/pH)
  • pH effect
  • Glycol / inhibitor efficiency
  • Flow velocity / shear stress (erosion-corrosion)

Usage example: 

 
 DeWaardMilliamsCorrosion model = new DeWaardMilliamsCorrosion();
 model.setTemperatureCelsius(60.0);
 model.setCO2PartialPressure(2.0); // bar
 model.setPH(4.5);
 model.setInhibitorEfficiency(0.80);
 double rate = model.calculateCorrosionRate(); // mm/yr
Version:
1.0
Author:
ESOL
See Also:

      public DeWaardMilliamsCorrosion()
      Creates a new DeWaardMilliamsCorrosion with default parameters.

      public DeWaardMilliamsCorrosion(double temperatureC, double co2PartialPressureBar)
      Creates a new DeWaardMilliamsCorrosion with specified conditions.
      Parameters:
      temperatureC - temperature in Celsius
      co2PartialPressureBar - CO2 partial pressure in bar

  • Method Details

    • setTemperatureCelsius

      public void setTemperatureCelsius(double temperatureC)
      Sets the temperature.
      Parameters:
      temperatureC - temperature in Celsius

    • setCO2PartialPressure

      public void setCO2PartialPressure(double pressureBar)
      Sets the CO2 partial pressure.
      Parameters:
      pressureBar - CO2 partial pressure in bar

    • setPH

      public void setPH(double pH)
      Sets the pH value.
      Parameters:
      pH - pH of the aqueous phase (typically 3.5 to 6.5)

    • setTotalPressure

      public void setTotalPressure(double pressureBar)
      Sets the total system pressure.
      Parameters:
      pressureBar - total pressure in bara

    • setH2SPartialPressure

      public void setH2SPartialPressure(double pressureBar)
      Sets the H2S partial pressure.
      Parameters:
      pressureBar - H2S partial pressure in bar

    • setFlowVelocity

      public void setFlowVelocity(double velocityMs)
      Sets the flow velocity.
      Parameters:
      velocityMs - flow velocity in m/s

    • setPipeDiameter

      public void setPipeDiameter(double diameterM)
      Sets the pipe internal diameter.
      Parameters:
      diameterM - internal diameter in metres

    • setInhibitorEfficiency

      public void setInhibitorEfficiency(double efficiency)
      Sets the chemical inhibitor efficiency.
      Parameters:
      efficiency - inhibitor efficiency (0.0 = none, 1.0 = perfect)

    • setGlycolFraction

      public void setGlycolFraction(double fraction)
      Sets the glycol weight fraction in the aqueous phase.
      Parameters:
      fraction - glycol weight fraction (0 to 1)

    • setSulfurDepositionRate

      public void setSulfurDepositionRate(double rateKgM2Yr)
      Sets the elemental sulfur (S8) mass deposition rate on the pipe wall.

      Elemental sulfur reacts directly with carbon steel: S8 + 8Fe = 8FeS. This provides an additional corrosion mechanism beyond CO2 and H2S. The reaction rate depends on the mass flux of sulfur particles reaching the wall.

      Parameters:
      rateKgM2Yr - sulfur deposition rate in kg/(m2*yr)

    • calculateBaselineRate

      public double calculateBaselineRate()
      Calculates the baseline corrosion rate using de Waard-Milliams 1991. 
       
 log10(Vcor) = 5.8 - 1710 / (T + 273.15) + 0.67 * log10(pCO2)
      Returns:
      baseline corrosion rate in mm/yr

    • getPHCorrectionFactor

      public double getPHCorrectionFactor()
      Calculates the pH correction factor.

      The pH correction from de Waard-Lotz (1993) reduces the corrosion rate at higher pH due to protective FeCO3 film formation: 

       
 f_pH = 10 ^ (0.32 * (pH_sat - pH_actual))

      where pH_sat is the saturation pH for FeCO3 at the given conditions. For pH above approximately 5.0 and temperature above 60 C, significant protection occurs.

      Returns:
      pH correction factor (less than or equal to 1 when pH is high)

    • calculateSaturationPH

      private double calculateSaturationPH()
      Calculates the FeCO3 saturation pH estimate.

      Simplified from NORSOK M-506: pH_sat depends on temperature and CO2 partial pressure.

      Returns:
      estimated saturation pH

    • getScaleCorrectionFactor

      public double getScaleCorrectionFactor()
      Calculates the protective scale correction factor.

      At temperatures above approximately 60-80 C and adequate pH, protective FeCO3 scale forms which reduces corrosion rate. The maximum corrosion rate occurs around 60-80 C; above this the scale becomes protective.

      Returns:
      scale correction factor (less than 1 at high temperature/pH)

    • getGlycolCorrectionFactor

      public double getGlycolCorrectionFactor()
      Calculates the glycol correction factor.

      Glycol in the aqueous phase reduces water activity and thus corrosion rate. The correction is approximately (1 - glycol fraction) for low glycol concentrations; for high glycol (above 80 wt%), the rate drops significantly.

      Returns:
      glycol correction factor (0 to 1)

    • getFlowCorrectionFactor

      public double getFlowCorrectionFactor()
      Calculates the flow velocity correction factor.

      At higher flow velocities, mass transfer of CO2 to the steel surface increases and protective scale (FeCO3) can be removed by wall shear stress. Based on the de Waard-Lotz flow-dependent factor using wall shear stress.

      Returns:
      flow correction factor (greater than or equal to 1)

    • getSulfurCorrosionRate

      public double getSulfurCorrosionRate()
      Calculates the corrosion rate contribution from elemental sulfur (S8) deposition.

      Elemental sulfur reacts directly with carbon steel: S8 + 8Fe = 8FeS + products. The corrosion rate depends on the mass flux of sulfur particles reaching the pipe wall. The stoichiometric ratio is 1 mol S8 (256.5 g) reacts with 8 mol Fe (447 g), giving a mass conversion factor of approximately 1.74 kg Fe corroded per kg S8 deposited.

      Returns:
      sulfur-induced corrosion rate in mm/yr

    • getFeCO3SaturationIndex

      public double getFeCO3SaturationIndex()
      Estimates the FeCO3 (siderite) scale formation tendency.

      FeCO3 forms when the ionic product of Fe2+ and CO3-2 exceeds the solubility product. At higher temperatures (> 60 C) and pH (> 5), the scale is protective and slows corrosion.

      Returns:
      FeCO3 saturation index estimate (greater than 1 indicates scale-forming conditions)

    • getFeSCorrosionRate

      public double getFeSCorrosionRate()
      Estimates the FeS (iron sulfide) corrosion rate from H2S.

      Uses the simplified Smith-de Waard sour corrosion model: ln(CR) = 7.96 - 2320/T + 0.67 * ln(pH2S) where CR is in mm/yr, T in K, pH2S in bar.

      Returns:
      H2S/FeS corrosion rate in mm/yr

    • getFeSScaleMorphology

      public String getFeSScaleMorphology()
      Returns the FeS scale morphology description based on temperature.

      The morphology determines whether the FeS scale provides corrosion protection:

      • Below 60 C: mackinawite (non-protective, porous)
      • 60-120 C: cubic FeS / troilite (partially protective)
      • Above 120 C: pyrrhotite (protective, dense)
      Returns:
      description of the FeS morphology and protectiveness

    • getComprehensiveAssessment

      public Map<String,Object> getComprehensiveAssessment()
      Returns a comprehensive corrosion assessment combining CO2, H2S, and S8 contributions.
      Returns:
      map of corrosion assessment results

    • calculateCorrosionRate

      public double calculateCorrosionRate()
      Calculates the fully corrected corrosion rate.

      The corrected rate is: Vcor_corrected = Vcor_base * f_pH * f_scale * f_glycol * f_flow * (1 - IE) + Vcor_sulfur

      Returns:
      corrected corrosion rate in mm/yr

    • calculateOverTemperatureRange

      public List<Map<String,Object>> calculateOverTemperatureRange(double minTempC, double maxTempC, int steps)
      Calculates corrosion rates over a range of temperatures.
      Parameters:
      minTempC - minimum temperature in Celsius
      maxTempC - maximum temperature in Celsius
      steps - number of temperature steps
      Returns:
      list of maps containing temperature and corrosion rate

    • getCorrosionSeverity

      public String getCorrosionSeverity()
      Determines the corrosion severity category.

      Categories per NORSOK M-001:

      • Low: less than 0.1 mm/yr
      • Medium: 0.1 to 0.3 mm/yr
      • High: 0.3 to 1.0 mm/yr
      • Very High: greater than 1.0 mm/yr
      Returns:
      severity category string

    • estimateCorrosionAllowance

      public double estimateCorrosionAllowance(double designLifeYears)
      Estimates the required corrosion allowance for a given design life.
      Parameters:
      designLifeYears - pipeline design life in years
      Returns:
      corrosion allowance in mm

    • isSourService

      public boolean isSourService()
      Checks whether H2S content exceeds sweet/sour threshold.

      NACE MR0175 / ISO 15156 defines sour service when H2S partial pressure exceeds 0.003 bar (0.05 psia) at total pressure, or H2S concentration exceeds 50 mg/L in water.

      Returns:
      true if service is sour per NACE MR0175

    • toJson

      public String toJson()
      Returns a comprehensive JSON report.
      Returns:
      JSON string with all corrosion parameters and results
